answer.text: How industry structure influences the conduct of firms which in turn determines industry performance
Only the internal operations of a single company

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The SCP model examines how industry structure (concentration, barriers, product differentiation) influences firm conduct (pricing, advertising, R&D) which determines performance (profitability, efficiency). **The Trap:** SCP is about the link between market structure, firm behavior, and outcomes — not just price performance or internal operations. **Cross-Exam Context:** SCP analysis framework tested in NISM Series XV Chapter 6. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** SCP suggests that market structure determines behavior: monopoly leads to price-setting conduct, while perfect competition leads to price-taking.
